title: Reuniting with a High School Best Friend

all.en: It had been ten long years since I last saw Emily, my best friend from high school.
all.zh: 距離我上次見到高中最好的朋友艾蜜莉，已經整整十年了。

all.en: We agreed to meet at our old favorite café in the town where we grew up.
all.zh: 我們約在從小長大的鎮上、那家以前最愛的咖啡館見面。

all.en: I arrived early and chose a seat by the window, my heart beating with excitement.
all.zh: 我提早抵達，挑了一個靠窗的位子，心跳因興奮而加快。

all.en: When she walked in, I almost didn't recognize her at first because of her short hair.
all.zh: 當她走進來時，因為她剪了短髮，我一開始幾乎認不出她。

all.en: We hugged each other tightly and laughed until tears came to our eyes.
all.zh: 我們緊緊擁抱彼此，笑到眼角都泛起了淚水。

all.en: She told me about her marriage, her two children, and her new career as a nurse.
all.zh: 她告訴我她的婚姻、兩個孩子，以及她身為護理師的新工作。

all.en: In return, I shared stories about my travels and the small business I had started.
all.zh: 作為回報，我也分享了我的旅行故事，以及我創立的小型事業。

all.en: We pulled out our old yearbook and pointed at silly photos of our teenage selves.
all.zh: 我們翻出舊的畢業紀念冊，指著我們青少年時期那些傻氣的照片。

all.en: Time seemed to stand still as we recalled our teachers, classmates, and embarrassing moments.
all.zh: 當我們回憶起老師、同學以及尷尬的時刻時，時間彷彿靜止了。

all.en: Although our lives had taken very different paths, the bond between us still felt strong.
all.zh: 雖然我們的人生走上了非常不同的道路，但我們之間的情誼依然深厚。

all.en: Before leaving, we promised to meet again soon and never lose touch for so long.
all.zh: 離開前，我們約定要儘快再見面，再也不要這麼久沒聯絡。

all.en: After our reunion, Emily and I started a monthly video call to keep our promise.
all.zh: 重逢之後，艾蜜莉和我開始每月一次視訊通話，以遵守我們的承諾。

all.en: She introduced me to her husband and her two energetic children on the screen.
all.zh: 她在螢幕上向我介紹了她的丈夫和兩個活力十足的孩子。

all.en: They drew colorful pictures for me and asked many questions about Taiwan.
all.zh: 他們為我畫了色彩繽紛的圖畫，並問了許多關於台灣的問題。

all.en: Six months later, I flew to her town for her younger daughter's birthday party.
all.zh: 六個月後，我飛到她的鎮上，參加她小女兒的生日派對。

all.en: She had decorated the garden with paper lanterns and balloons in bright colors.
all.zh: 她用紙燈籠和顏色鮮豔的氣球裝飾了整座花園。

all.en: We baked a strawberry cake together, just like we used to do in high school.
all.zh: 我們一起烤了一個草莓蛋糕，就像我們高中時常做的那樣。

all.en: That night, after the children fell asleep, we sat on the porch with warm tea.
all.zh: 那晚孩子們睡著後，我們坐在門廊上，捧著溫熱的茶。

all.en: She admitted that life had been harder than she ever expected as a young adult.
all.zh: 她坦承成年後的生活比她預期中還要艱辛許多。

all.en: I told her that no matter what happened, I would always be just one call away.
all.zh: 我告訴她無論發生什麼事，我永遠都只在一通電話之外。

all.en: Our friendship had survived ten silent years and now felt stronger than ever before.
all.zh: 我們的友誼度過了沉默的十年，如今感覺比以往任何時候都更堅韌。

all.en: After my visit, Emily and I began writing handwritten letters to each other every few months.
all.zh: 那次拜訪之後，艾蜜莉和我開始每隔幾個月就互相寫手寫信。

all.en: Her envelopes always carried small dried flowers from her garden tucked inside the pages.
all.zh: 她的信封裡總是夾著從她花園採來的小小乾燥花。

all.en: I saved every letter in a wooden box my grandfather had made decades ago.
all.zh: 我把每一封信都收進一個我祖父幾十年前親手做的木盒裡。

all.en: During winter break, Emily flew to Taiwan with her older daughter for the very first time.
all.zh: 寒假期間，艾蜜莉第一次帶著她的大女兒飛來台灣。

all.en: I took them to night markets, mountain temples, and a quiet beach near my hometown.
all.zh: 我帶她們去夜市、山上的寺廟，還有我家鄉附近一片寧靜的海灘。

all.en: Her daughter tasted bubble tea for the first time and asked for three cups in one day.
all.zh: 她女兒第一次喝珍珠奶茶，一天內就喝了三杯。

all.en: Emily and I cooked beef noodle soup together using my mother's old family recipe.
all.zh: 艾蜜莉和我用我媽媽家傳的老食譜，一起煮了牛肉麵。

all.en: On their last night, we sat under the stars and made a list of future trips.
all.zh: 她們在台灣的最後一晚，我們坐在星空下，列了一份未來旅行的清單。

all.en: We promised to alternate countries every two years so the bond would never weaken again.
all.zh: 我們約定每兩年輪流前往對方的國家，這樣感情就不會再次淡掉。

all.en: Sometimes the deepest friendships are the ones we choose to keep choosing every year.
all.zh: 有時候最深厚的友誼，是我們每一年都選擇繼續經營的那一段。

all.en: The following autumn, Emily and I decided to start a small annual scholarship in our hometown.
all.zh: 隔年秋天，艾蜜莉和我決定在我們的家鄉設立一個小型的年度獎學金。

all.en: We wanted to support quiet students who loved books but rarely raised their hands in class.
all.zh: 我們想支持那些熱愛書本卻很少在課堂上舉手的安靜學生。

all.en: Our old high school teacher helped us design a simple application form and a fair process.
all.zh: 我們以前的高中老師幫忙設計了簡單的申請表和公平的審查流程。

all.en: On the day of the first interviews, Emily flew in despite a heavy work schedule.
all.zh: 第一次面試當天，艾蜜莉儘管工作行程繁重，仍然飛了過來。

all.en: We met three thoughtful candidates whose stories reminded us strongly of our younger selves.
all.zh: 我們會見了三位有想法的候選人，他們的故事讓我們強烈地想起年少時的自己。

all.en: The chosen student was a shy girl who wanted to become a translator someday.
all.zh: 獲選的是一位害羞的女孩，她希望有朝一日成為一位翻譯。

all.en: Emily cried quietly during the announcement, holding my hand under the wooden ceremony table.
all.zh: 頒獎時艾蜜莉靜靜地哭了，在木製典禮桌下緊握著我的手。

all.en: That evening, we revisited our old café and toasted with hot chocolate just like teenagers.
all.zh: 那晚，我們重返舊咖啡館，像青少年時期一樣以熱巧克力相互敬酒。

all.en: We promised to expand the scholarship every year until ten students could benefit each round.
all.zh: 我們約定每年都要擴大獎學金規模，直到每一屆能有十位學生受惠。

all.en: Our friendship had finally become something larger than ourselves, and that felt absolutely right.
all.zh: 我們的友誼終於成為比我們自己更大的存在，那種感覺再正確不過了。

all.en: The scholarship's second year attracted twice as many applications from quiet students across our hometown.
all.zh: 獎學金的第二年吸引了來自家鄉各地兩倍之多的安靜學生來申請。

all.en: Emily and I read every essay carefully and discussed each candidate over long evening phone calls.
all.zh: 艾蜜莉和我仔細閱讀每一篇文章，並在漫長的夜間電話中討論每位候選人。

all.en: We chose three new recipients, including a quiet boy who dreamed of becoming a veterinarian someday.
all.zh: 我們選出了三位新得主，其中包括一位夢想有朝一日成為獸醫的安靜男孩。

all.en: Our local newspaper wrote a warm feature about two friends keeping their teenage promise alive together.
all.zh: 當地報紙寫了一篇溫暖的專題報導，談兩位朋友如何一起守護青少年時期的諾言。

all.en: Several retired teachers contacted us, offering to mentor recipients through their first university years.
all.zh: 幾位退休教師聯絡我們，願意在獎學金得主的大學初期擔任他們的導師。

all.en: Emily flew over again, and we hosted a small mentor-matching event at our old high school library.
all.zh: 艾蜜莉再次飛來，我們在以前的高中圖書館舉辦了一場小型的導師媒合活動。

all.en: The first scholar, now a college sophomore, returned to volunteer and warmly welcome the new students.
all.zh: 第一位得主如今已是大學二年級，回來擔任志工，並熱情地歡迎新進的學生。

all.en: She told everyone that the scholarship had given her courage long before it gave her tuition.
all.zh: 她告訴大家，這筆獎學金早在給她學費之前，就已先給了她勇氣。

all.en: Emily squeezed my hand quietly under the table, and I saw tears glisten on her smiling cheeks.
all.zh: 艾蜜莉在桌下悄悄握住我的手，我看見淚水在她微笑的臉頰上閃爍。

all.en: Our teenage promise, once whispered in a café, now echoed through many young, hopeful lives.
all.zh: 我們青少年時期在咖啡館低語的承諾，如今在許多年輕而充滿希望的生命中迴響。

all.en: By the fifth year, our scholarship had supported fifteen students across two quiet rural districts.
all.zh: 到了第五年，我們的獎學金已資助兩個寧靜鄉村學區共十五位學生。

all.en: Emily and I decided to organize an annual gathering for past, current, and incoming scholars together.
all.zh: 艾蜜莉和我決定為歷屆、現任與新進得主舉辦一場年度聚會。

all.en: We chose a community hall near our old high school and decorated it with simple paper lanterns.
all.zh: 我們選了舊高中附近的社區活動中心，並用簡單的紙燈籠裝飾。

all.en: On the day of the gathering, more than thirty people arrived, including parents and former teachers.
all.zh: 聚會當天有超過三十人到場，包括家長和昔日的老師。

all.en: Our first recipient, now a working translator, gave a quiet speech that moved the whole room.
all.zh: 我們的第一位得主如今已是一名執業翻譯，她那段安靜的演講感動了全場。

all.en: She thanked Emily and me for trusting her dream before she even fully trusted it herself.
all.zh: 她感謝艾蜜莉和我在她自己都還不完全相信夢想之前，就先選擇了相信她。

all.en: Several alumni offered to fund part of next year's scholarship from their own modest salaries.
all.zh: 幾位歷屆得主主動表示，願意從自己微薄的薪水中資助一部分明年的獎學金。

all.en: Emily and I looked at each other across the room, both quietly fighting back proud tears.
all.zh: 艾蜜莉和我從房間兩端對望，都默默強忍著驕傲的淚水。

all.en: That night, we walked along the river where we used to ride bicycles as restless teenagers.
all.zh: 那晚，我們沿著河邊散步，那是我們青少年時期常騎單車的不安年代所走過的路。

all.en: We agreed that friendship, when carefully tended, becomes a quiet garden that feeds many generations.
all.zh: 我們一致認為，用心經營的友誼，會成為一座滋養許多世代的寧靜花園。

all.en: The next morning, Emily and I visited our old high school and walked through every quiet corridor.
all.zh: 隔天早上，艾蜜莉和我重訪舊高中，走過每一條寧靜的走廊。

all.en: We stopped at our former lockers and noticed how small they looked compared to our memories.
all.zh: 我們在以前的置物櫃前停下，發覺它們比記憶中看起來小了許多。

all.en: Our former English teacher invited us to speak to her current class about following meaningful dreams.
all.zh: 我們以前的英文老師邀請我們向她現在的班級談談如何追隨有意義的夢想。

all.en: Emily told the students that fear and hope often live together inside one quiet teenage heart.
all.zh: 艾蜜莉告訴學生們，恐懼與希望經常同住在一顆安靜的青少年心裡。

all.en: I shared how one promise between best friends had quietly shaped years of our adult lives.
all.zh: 我分享了兩個摯友之間的一個承諾，如何悄悄形塑了我們成年後的許多歲月。

all.en: After class, a shy girl handed us a folded note asking how she could apply someday.
all.zh: 下課後，一個害羞的女孩遞給我們一張摺起的小紙條，問她有朝一日該如何申請。

all.en: We answered her gently, sensing in her cautious eyes the same wishes we once carried inside.
all.zh: 我們溫柔地回答她，從她謹慎的眼神中察覺到我們昔日所懷抱的同樣心願。

all.en: Walking back to the café that afternoon, the autumn wind felt like an old, familiar friend.
all.zh: 那天下午走回咖啡館的路上，秋風感覺像一位久違而熟悉的老朋友。

all.en: Emily said simply that this work would outlast us, and I quietly agreed without needing words.
all.zh: 艾蜜莉只簡單地說，這份工作會比我們活得更久，而我不需多言便靜靜地點頭同意。

all.en: We sat by the window again, ten years older than before, but somehow younger inside ourselves.
all.zh: 我們再次坐在窗邊，比十年前蒼老，但內心卻彷彿更加年輕。

all.en: By the seventh year, our scholarship had quietly become a respected program across our small county.
all.zh: 到了第七年，我們的獎學金已悄悄成為我們小郡裡備受敬重的計畫。

all.en: Emily and I formalized our partnership and opened a small office above the old hometown café.
all.zh: 艾蜜莉和我正式立案合作，並在家鄉舊咖啡館樓上開了一間小型辦公室。

all.en: We hired our first scholarship alumna as a part-time program coordinator during her quiet graduate years.
all.zh: 我們聘請了第一位獎學金校友，在她安靜的研究所歲月中擔任兼職計畫協調員。

all.en: She built a gentle alumni network connecting every past recipient through monthly online conversations together.
all.zh: 她建立了一個溫柔的校友網絡，透過每月的線上對談把每位歷屆得主連結起來。

all.en: Several alumni now run their own small projects supporting quiet children in rural villages nearby.
all.zh: 好幾位校友如今正經營自己的小型計畫，支持附近鄉村寧靜的孩童。

all.en: Emily flies in three times a year, and we always reserve one quiet morning for old café tea.
all.zh: 艾蜜莉每年飛來三次，我們總會保留一個安靜的早晨，到老咖啡館喝茶。

all.en: Her older daughter began volunteering with us last summer, reviewing applications beside her mother and me.
all.zh: 她的大女兒去年夏天開始與我們一起擔任志工，與她媽媽和我一同審閱申請。

all.en: On our tenth anniversary, the city dedicated a small bench to us at our old high school garden.
all.zh: 在十週年那天，城市在我們舊高中的花園裡為我們設立了一張小巧的長椅。

all.en: We sat on it together at sunset, listening as students walked past laughing in the gentle evening light.
all.zh: 夕陽時分我們一同坐在長椅上，聽著學生們在溫柔的暮光中笑著經過。

all.en: Friendship, Emily whispered softly, is the only quiet inheritance we ever truly leave behind for others.
all.zh: 艾蜜莉輕聲說，友誼是我們真正能為他人留下的、唯一安靜的傳承。

all.en: Last week, Emily sent me a photo of her teenage daughter wearing my old high school graduation jacket.
all.zh: 上週艾蜜莉傳給我一張照片，是她青少年的女兒穿著我以前高中畢業外套的樣子。

all.en: We discovered we still both remembered every word to that silly cafeteria song from twelfth grade.
all.zh: 我們發現兩個人到現在還記得高三餐廳裡那首傻氣歌曲的每一句歌詞。

all.en: My husband once asked why Emily and I texted in such weird inside jokes, and I just smiled.
all.zh: 我先生有次問我為什麼我和艾蜜莉的訊息都是奇怪的內梗，我只笑而不答。

all.en: Yesterday, I accidentally called Emily by our old high school nickname during a serious work meeting.
all.zh: 昨天我在嚴肅的工作會議中，不小心用我們高中的綽號叫了艾蜜莉。

all.en: She once mailed me a packet of seeds from her garden, and I now have her tomato plants on my balcony.
all.zh: 她曾寄了一包她花園的種子給我，現在我的陽台上種著她那邊的番茄。

all.en: Last Christmas, we mailed each other identical fluffy socks without planning, and laughed for almost an hour straight.
all.zh: 去年聖誕節，我們完全沒商量卻互寄了一模一樣的毛茸茸襪子，大笑了將近一個小時。

all.en: My phone autocompletes her name whenever I type the letter E, which secretly makes me smile every time.
all.zh: 我手機只要打到 E 就會自動補完她的名字，這件事每次都讓我偷偷會心一笑。

all.en: She remembers my mother's birthday and always sends a card before I can even remind her about it.
all.zh: 她記得我媽的生日，每次都還沒等我提醒，卡片就已經先寄到了。

all.en: Sometimes we just video-call silently while doing laundry, and that quiet companionship feels surprisingly priceless to me.
all.zh: 有時候我們只是靜靜視訊著各自折衣服，那份安靜的陪伴對我來說意外地珍貴。

all.en: Honestly, I think the ten silent years made us appreciate this version of our friendship so much more.
all.zh: 老實說，我覺得就是那沉默的十年，讓我們更珍惜現在這個版本的友情。
